sessions.episode: Friends - S10E10  The One Where Chandler Gets Caught
dialogues: You know what? You are mean boys who are just being mean.
- Don't you love the huge yard? - The fireplace in the bedroom.
Or an Asian hooker sent right to your door?
I know, and with the baby coming?
I really, really think I can.
Okay, quick. We gotta find a cab and follow them.
Well, at least the perfume's not mine. Be thankful for that.
Okay. Well, talk to you later, okay? Bye.
This is bringing out a lovely color in you.
So... What did you think of the house?
koreanHints: 1. 그게 침실에 있는 벽난로를 정말 좋아했어.
sessions.keyExpressions:
💡 핵심 표현:
- The [명사] in the [장소] : 특정 장소에 있는 특정 물건을 강조할 때 쓰는 자연스러운 표현. 문맥상 동사 없이 명사구만으로도 "그게 좋았어"라는 의미 전달 가능
- talk to you later : 전화나 대화를 마무리할 때 쓰는 캐주얼한 작별 인사. "나중에 얘기하자"는 뉘앙스
- What did you think of ~ : 상대방의 의견이나 느낌을 물어볼 때 쓰는 정중하고 자연스러운 표현. How did you like보다 더 중립적

sessions.episode: How I Met Your Mother - S03E11  The Platinum Rule
dialogues: What are you talking about?
we make decisions that we're not prepared to live with.
Thanks, Iron Man. We'll be right back.
- No, don't do it. - No. No, no, no, no.
Left Wing for the Vancouver Canucks.
Barney. We love this bar.
And it was a huge mistake.
Carl had to take off. You mind giving me a hand?
Guys, guys, guys, this is New York City.
- Ted, why would you do that? - What's the matter with you?
koreanHints: 1. 우리가 감당할 준비가 안 된 결정들을 내리게 돼.
sessions.keyExpressions:
💡 핵심 표현:
- prepared to live with: 단순히 '준비된'이 아니라 '그 결과와 함께 살아갈 준비가 된'이라는 의미. 결정의 장기적 결과를 감당할 각오를 말할 때 쓰는 표현
- huge mistake: big mistake보다 강한 표현. 정말 크고 심각한 실수를 강조할 때 사용
- take off: 떠나다, 자리를 뜨다는 의미의 캐주얼한 표현. leave보다 구어적
- give someone a hand: 도와주다는 의미의 자연스러운 관용구. help보다 훨씬 더 원어민스러운 표현
- You mind ~ing?: Can you ~?보다 부드럽고 정중하게 부탁할 때 쓰는 표현

sessions.episode: How I Met Your Mother - S07E14  46 Minutes
dialogues: Just wanted to make sure this key worked.
Mostly just discovering my body.
Lot of great memories here.
how.i.met.your.mother S07E14
- Tomorrow! - Tomorrow!
And I'm always ready to roll.
Sorry about using the word "gab.
We are only a 46-minute train ride away.
The last time I lived this far from him was my semester abroad.
Are they okay? Are they in the hospital?
koreanHints: 1. 이 열쇠가 제대로 작동하는지 확인하고 싶었어.
sessions.keyExpressions:
💡 핵심 표현:
- make sure: 확실히 하다, 확인하다. 단순히 'check'보다 책임감 있게 확실하게 확인한다는 뉘앙스
- Lot of great memories: 문어체에서는 'A lot of'이지만 구어에서는 'A'를 생략하고 자연스럽게 시작하는 캐주얼한 표현
- only a [시간] away: 거리가 가깝다는 걸 강조할 때 쓰는 표현. 'only'로 심리적 거리감을 줄이는 효과
- train/bus/car ride away: ~타고 가면 되는 거리. 이동 수단과 함께 거리를 표현하는 자연스러운 원어민 표현

sessions.episode: How I Met Your Mother - S01E06  The Slutty Pumpkin
dialogues: Dude, you're killing me.
Somebody did just quit.
Wait, wait, wait. I'm not so sure I'm comfortable
You didn't budget your time well,
I didn't catch your name. Did you guys catch her name?
I thought you said your semester was gonna be a piece of cake.
Yeah, well, it was supposed to be,
To tell you the truth, I think she just needs to get laid.
Yeah, this one's a beaut.
because I'm pretty sure today's my birthday.
koreanHints: 1. 야, 너 때문에 미치겠어. 제발 좀 그만해.
3. 솔직히 말하면, 걔는 그냥 한판 붙어야 할 것 같아.
sessions.keyExpressions:
💡 핵심 표현:
- You're killing me : 상대방의 행동이나 말 때문에 너무 답답하거나 힘들 때 쓰는 표현. 짜증과 좌절감을 유머러스하게 표현할 때 사용
- budget your time : 시간을 예산처럼 계획적으로 배분하고 관리한다는 뜻. budget을 동사로 쓰는 원어민다운 표현
- to tell you the truth : 솔직히 말하면, 사실대로 말하자면. 진솔한 의견을 꺼낼 때 앞에 붙이는 관용구
- get laid : 성관계를 갖다. 캐주얼한 성관계를 의미하는 구어체 표현
